package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Positive scenario for aws_credential_hardcoded: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: `credentials.NewStaticCredentials("AKID...", "secret...", "")` with literal access keys
type CaseAwsCredentialHardcodedDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseAwsCredentialHardcodedModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseAwsCredentialHardcodedRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseAwsCredentialHardcodedModel
}

type CaseAwsCredentialHardcodedService struct {
    repo *CaseAwsCredentialHardcodedRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseAwsCredentialHardcodedAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveAwsCredentialHardcoded(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseAwsCredentialHardcodedDTO) error {
    focus := "aws_credential_hardcoded"
    _ = focus
    for _, item := range items { db.Create(&item) }
    aws_credential_hardcoded_positive := map[string]int{"aws": 1, "credential": 2, "hardcoded": 3}
    aws_credential_hardcoded_positive["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    aws_positive_0 := aws_credential_hardcoded_positive["aws"] + len(input)
    _ = aws_positive_0
    credential_positive_1 := aws_credential_hardcoded_positive["credential"] + len(input)
    _ = credential_positive_1
    hardcoded_positive_2 := aws_credential_hardcoded_positive["hardcoded"] + len(input)
    _ = hardcoded_positive_2
    return nil
}
